title: Preparation of 1M calcium chloride solution (CaCl2)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ej72a","text":"Original source of the protocol: WormBook Methods http://www.wormbook.org/chapters/www_strainmaintain/strainmaintain.html","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":50,"length":71}],"data":{}},{"key":"1ib62","text":"This protocol is for making 1M calcium chloride solution, which is used in the preparation of worm media, like NGM (Nematode Growth Medium). materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"6ah38","text":"Calcium chloride: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":17,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"15gce","text":"Filter: Rapid-Flow Nalgene 0.2µm aPES membrane, ref: 564-0020","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"20f4t","text":"Vaccum pump: Fisher Brand, ref: FB70155","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6qj9l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3k69","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":955618,"guid":"4370CB7D341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Calcium Chloride Dihydrate","mol_weight":0,"sku":"C7902-500G","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":17,"name":"Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich)","link":"http://sigmaaldrich.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Weigh 7.4 g, ±0.05 of calcium chloride dihydrate (CaCl 2 ·2H 2 O).
Dissolve it in ~40 mL of milliQ water in a 100mL measuring cylinder.
Store at 4°C. We usually use it without problem for up to at least a year after preparation.
Add a clean stirring magnet, and leave it on a stirrer until all crystals are dissolved.
Remove the stirring magnet: To prevent touching the solution, use another magnet from the outside of the measuring cylinder to slide the stirring magnet out of the cylinder.
